meaning: n.兄弟关系,友爱,互助会,兄弟会
examples: Bob needs the fraternity of others who share his mission.
He must have been the most popular guy at the most popular fraternity in college.
They would like the college administration to exert more control over the fraternity.
In one scene, the monsters are dancing and acting wild at a fraternity party.
None of that would have surprised Baffert, or any of the other members of the fraternity.
